message:
fix(pager): stop unconditional downgrade of RESTART/TRUNCATE checkpoints (#66)

The pager unconditionally downgraded RESTART and TRUNCATE checkpoint
modes to FULL, preventing the WAL file from ever being truncated. This
caused unbounded WAL growth in single-connection workloads where
truncation is perfectly safe.

Four fixes, ordered by safety:

1. Remove stale KnownGap in wal_journal_parity.rs that claimed
   auto-checkpoint was not triggered after commits. It IS triggered,
   as proven by existing test
   test_pragma_checkpoint_autocheckpoint_triggers_and_disable_semantics.

2. Add single-connection fast path in Pager::checkpoint: when
   shared_connection_count == 1 and active_transactions == 0 (already
   enforced), there are no external readers, so RESTART and TRUNCATE
   are safe to execute without reader-tracking. Multi-connection
   setups still conservatively downgrade to FULL.

3. Allow checkpoint_pick_auto_mode to choose Truncate when
   active_txn_count == 0 and the WAL exceeds 2x the urgent threshold.
   The pager's single-connection fast path allows this through for
   sole connections; multi-connection setups get downgraded to Full.

4. Surface the downgrade in CheckpointResult via new requested_mode
   and effective_mode fields, so callers (export_database_bytes,
   copy_database_to, PRAGMA wal_checkpoint) can detect when their
   requested mode was not honored.

Closes #66

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
